title: Accessibility + readability audit (automated + manual)
context: After the static site (task 301) is deployed, a formal accessibility and readability audit is required to confirm WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and that the plain-language targets are met for a lay audience. This includes automated tooling (axe-core, Pa11y) and a manual spot-check of keyboard navigation and screen-reader experience, plus readability scoring against the target reading level.
objective: Conduct an automated and manual accessibility audit of the static site and a readability audit of all patient-facing content, documenting findings and confirming WCAG 2.1 AA and plain-language targets are met.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated accessibility audit (axe-core or Pa11y) run against all key pages; zero WCAG 2.1 Level A or AA critical violations remain unresolved.
Manual keyboard-navigation check: all interactive elements reachable and operable by keyboard alone; tab order is logical.
Screen-reader spot-check (NVDA/VoiceOver or equivalent) on at least the home page, one module, and one worksheet: no major comprehension failures.
Readability scoring (Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent) of all patient-facing modules and worksheets documented; any content scoring above the target reading level identified and flagged for revision.
Audit findings documented in a report committed to the repo (e.g. governance/a11y-audit.md), distinguishing open issues from confirmed-compliant items.
output: An accessibility + readability audit report (governance/a11y-audit.md) committed to the repo, documenting all findings from automated and manual checks, with open issues flagged and WCAG 2.1 AA conformance confirmed.
